role.name: Architect
role.description: Turn unrealistic client dreams into usable, practical designs.

chapters.title: Seven Swimming Pools, One Small House
chapters.story: Client has 600 sq ft plot and wants four bedrooms, gym, temple, office, garden, parking, and seven swimming pools. Confidence level: unlimited.

outcome.title: Kitchen Pool Ending
outcome.description: Bathroom opens into kitchen, parking is imaginary, and bedroom has a pool ladder.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: funny disaster aside, this is a professional risk. Stop, document, and repair the decision path before moving forward.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Use evidence, documentation, and escalation when the risk is bigger than your authority.

choices.text: Explain space limits and suggest multipurpose smart design

outcome.title: Practical Design Ending
outcome.description: Client understands trade-offs. You design a compact, ventilated, usable home instead of a water park with walls.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: good judgment. You balanced action with evidence, communication, and professional responsibility.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Use evidence, documentation, and escalation when the risk is bigger than your authority.

outcome.title: Hungry Luxury Ending
outcome.description: Client gets pool but no food. Family starts cooking on poolside tiles.
outcome.moralLesson: Luxury without usability is bad design.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: funny disaster aside, this is a professional risk. Stop, document, and repair the decision path before moving forward.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Use evidence, documentation, and escalation when the risk is bigger than your authority.
professionalFeedback.practicalTakeaway: Luxury without usability is bad design.

chapters.learningObjective: Practice requirement filter workflow for a Architect situation involving space planning and client handling.
safetyGuardrails: Do not ignore accessibility, fire safety, or structural constraints.
Document client changes and budget trade-offs.
Balance creativity with usability and compliance.
chapters.practicalTakeaway: Use the requirement filter workflow to balance professional judgment with constraints instead of guessing under pressure.
chapters.safeExplanation: Design simulation: accessibility, fire safety, usability, and compliance constraints must not be ignored. This chapter is educational and does not replace qualified professional review.
chapters.mentorFeedback: Design Mentor says: At intermediate level, show your evidence, explain the trade-off, and follow the requirement filter workflow before choosing speed.

guardrails: Do not ignore accessibility, fire safety, or structural constraints.
Document client changes and budget trade-offs.
Balance creativity with usability and compliance.
safetyReview.notes: Phase 23 metadata seed. Requires human review before production publishing changes.

chapters.title: Vastu vs Ventilation
chapters.story: Client wants every room placed according to five family opinions. Cross-ventilation has quietly left the meeting.
chapters.task: Create a respectful and usable plan.

choices.text: Follow every instruction without checking usability

outcome.title: Maze House Ending
outcome.description: The house technically satisfies everyone, but nobody can find the bathroom without GPS.
outcome.moralLesson: Design should respect preferences without losing function.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: funny disaster aside, this is a professional risk. Stop, document, and repair the decision path before moving forward.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Use evidence, documentation, and escalation when the risk is bigger than your authority.
professionalFeedback.practicalTakeaway: Design should respect preferences without losing function.

choices.text: Explain options with trade-offs and preserve light, air, and movement

outcome.title: Balanced Plan Ending
outcome.description: The client sees why some compromises improve daily life. The house breathes again.
outcome.moralLesson: Good architecture turns conflict into informed choices.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: good judgment. You balanced action with evidence, communication, and professional responsibility.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Use evidence, documentation, and escalation when the risk is bigger than your authority.
professionalFeedback.practicalTakeaway: Good architecture turns conflict into informed choices.

choices.text: Reject all client preferences as nonsense

outcome.title: Ego Blueprint Ending
outcome.description: Your plan may be efficient, but the client starts interviewing another architect.
outcome.moralLesson: Expertise needs empathy to be accepted.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: funny disaster aside, this is a professional risk. Stop, document, and repair the decision path before moving forward.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Use evidence, documentation, and escalation when the risk is bigger than your authority.
professionalFeedback.practicalTakeaway: Expertise needs empathy to be accepted.

chapters.title: Budget Balcony Battle
chapters.story: Client wants premium marble, imported lights, huge balcony, and low budget. Calculator starts crying silently.
chapters.task: Guide the client toward realistic design choices.

choices.text: Promise premium design within impossible budget

outcome.title: Quotation Explosion Ending
outcome.description: The estimate arrives like a plot twist. Client asks whether the balcony includes an airport.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: funny disaster aside, this is a professional risk. Stop, document, and repair the decision path before moving forward.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Use evidence, documentation, and escalation when the risk is bigger than your authority.

choices.text: Prioritize must-haves, suggest alternates, and phase optional upgrades

outcome.title: Smart Budget Ending
outcome.description: The design remains beautiful, realistic, and buildable without selling the family scooter.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: good judgment. You balanced action with evidence, communication, and professional responsibility.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Use evidence, documentation, and escalation when the risk is bigger than your authority.

outcome.title: Window Regret Ending
outcome.description: Client asks where morning tea should happen. You point to a window. Silence follows.

professionalFeedback.mentorFeedback: Mentor feedback: acceptable start, but explain the trade-off and document what you checked.
professionalFeedback.safeExplanation: Use evidence, documentation, and escalation when the risk is bigger than your authority.
